   11: const BASE_URL = 'https://api.pos.dutchie.com' // NO /v1 suffix
   12: const MAX_RETRIES = 4
   13: const REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000
   14: 
   15: export class DutchieClient {
   16:   private authHeader: string
   17: 
   18:   constructor(private apiKey: string) {
   19:     this.authHeader = `Basic ${Buffer.from(apiKey + ':').toString('base64')}`
   20:   }
   21: 
   22:   /**
   23:    * Core GET with rate limit retry + AbortSignal timeout.
   24:    * On 429: reads Retry-After header or uses exponential backoff 1sâ†’2sâ†’4sâ†’8s.
   25:    */
   26:   private async get<T>(path: string, params?: Record<string, string | number | boolean | undefined>): Promise<T> {
   27:     const url = new URL(`${BASE_URL}${path}`)
   28:     if (params) {
   29:       Object.entries(params).forEach(([k, v]) => {
   30:         if (v !== undefined) url.searchParams.set(k, String(v))
   31:       })
   32:     }
   33: 
   34:     let lastError: Error | null = null
   35:     for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= MAX_RETRIES; attempt++) {
   36:       try {
   37:         const start = Date.now()
   38:         const res = await fetch(url.toString(), {
   39:           method: 'GET',
   40:           headers: { Authorization: this.authHeader, Accept: 'application/json' },
   41:           signal: AbortSignal.timeout(REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS),
   42:         })
   43: 
   44:         if (res.status === 429) {
   45:           const retryAfter = res.headers.get('Retry-After')
   46:           const waitMs = retryAfter ? parseInt(retryAfter) * 1000 : Math.pow(2, attempt) * 1000
   47:           logger.warn('Dutchie rate limited', { path, attempt, waitMs })
   48:           await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, waitMs))
   49:           continue
   50:         }
   51: 
   52:         if (!res.ok) {
   53:           const text = await res.text().catch(() => '')
   54:           throw new Error(`Dutchie API ${res.status}: ${text || res.statusText}`)
   55:         }
   56: 
   57:         const data = await res.json()
   58:         logger.info('Dutchie request', { path, status: res.status, ms: Date.now() - start })
   59:         return data as T
   60:       } catch (err) {
   61:         lastError = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err))
   62:         if (attempt < MAX_RETRIES && (lastError.name === 'AbortError' || lastError.message.includes('429'))) {
   63:           await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, Math.pow(2, attempt) * 1000))
   64:           continue
   65:         }
   66:         throw lastError
   67:       }
   68:     }
   69:     throw lastError || new Error('Max retries exceeded')
   70:   }
   71: 
   72:   /**
   73:    * Unwraps responses that may be arrays or objects wrapping arrays.
   74:    */
   75:   private unwrap<T>(data: unknown): T[] {
   76:     if (Array.isArray(data)) return data
   77:     if (typeof data === 'object' && data !== null) {
   78:       const vals = Object.values(data)
   79:       const arr = vals.find(v => Array.isArray(v))
   80:       if (arr) return arr as T[]
  235:   /**
  236:    * The snapshot endpoint is limited to one request per minute. It deliberately
  237:    * bypasses the retrying GET wrapper so one sync run can issue exactly one HTTP
  238:    * attempt, including for 429 and timeout responses.
  239:    */
  240:   async getLoyaltySnapshot(): Promise<DutchieLoyaltySnapshot[]> {

  242:     const res = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}${path}`, {
  243:       method: 'GET',

The sync trace has exposed one data reversal risk and one lease race worth validating against tests: lookup errors in the pending loyalty staging path are silently treated as “no pending run,” and five minute leases are not heartbeated during potentially long full API fetches. I’m now applying the UI audit guidance for the codemod portion, focusing specifically on semantic class changes and interaction states rather than visual taste.

name: impeccable
description: Use when the user wants to design, redesign, shape, critique, audit, polish, clarify, distill, harden, optimize, adapt, animate, colorize, extract, or otherwise improve a frontend interface. Covers websites, landing pages, dashboards, product UI, app shells, components, forms, settings, onboarding, and empty states. Handles UX review, visual hierarchy, information architecture, cognitive load, accessibility, performance, responsive behavior, theming, anti-patterns, typography, fonts, spacing, layout, alignment, color, motion, micro-interactions, UX copy, error states, edge cases, i18n, and reusable design systems or tokens. Also use for bland designs that need to become bolder or more delightful, loud designs that should become quieter, live browser iteration on UI elements, or ambitious visual effects that should feel technically extraordinary. Not for backend-only or non-UI tasks.
version: 3.0.6
user-invocable: true
argument-hint: "[craft|shape Â· audit|critique Â· animate|bolder|colorize|delight|layout|overdrive|quieter|typeset Â· adapt|clarify|distill Â· harden|onboard|optimize|polish Â· teach|document|extract|live] [target]"
license: Apache 2.0. Based on Anthropic's frontend-design skill. See NOTICE.md for attribution.
allowed-tools:
  - Bash(npx impeccable *)
---

Designs and iterates production-grade frontend interfaces. Real working code, committed design choices, exceptional craft.

## Setup (non-optional)

Before any design work or file edits, pass these gates. Skipping them produces generic output that ignores the project.

| Gate | Required check | If fail |
|---|---|---|
| Context | The PRODUCT.md / DESIGN.md loader result is known from `node .Codex/skills/impeccable/scripts/load-context.mjs`. | Run the loader before continuing. |
| Product | PRODUCT.md exists and is not empty or placeholder (`[TODO]` markers, <200 chars). | Run `/impeccable teach`, refresh context, then resume. Never synthesize PRODUCT.md from the user's original prompt alone. |
| Command | The matching command reference is loaded when a sub-command is used. | Load the reference before continuing. |
| Craft | `/impeccable craft` has a user-confirmed shape brief for this task. `teach` / PRODUCT.md never counts as shape. | Run `/impeccable shape` and wait for explicit brief confirmation. |
| Image | Required visual probes / mocks are generated or skipped with a reason. | Resolve the image-generation gate in `shape.md` or `craft.md` before code. |
| Mutation | All active gates above pass. | Do not edit project files yet. |

Codex-style agents must state this before editing files:

```text
IMPECCABLE_PREFLIGHT: context=pass product=pass command_reference=pass shape=pass|not_required image_gate=pass|skipped:<reason> mutation=open
```

For `/impeccable craft`, `shape=pass` is only valid after a separate user response approving the shape design brief, or when the user provided an already-confirmed brief in the request. Do not mark `shape=pass` after writing PRODUCT.md, summarizing assumptions, or drafting an unconfirmed brief yourself.

Other harnesses should follow the same checklist when they can expose this state.

### 1. Context gathering

Two files, case-insensitive. The loader looks at the project root by default and falls back to `.agents/context/` and `docs/` if the root is clean. Override with `IMPECCABLE_CONTEXT_DIR=path/to/dir` (absolute or relative to cwd).

- **PRODUCT.md** â€” required. Users, brand, tone, anti-references, strategic principles.
- **DESIGN.md** â€” optional, strongly recommended. Colors, typography, elevation, components.

Load both in one call:

```bash
node .Codex/skills/impeccable/scripts/load-context.mjs
```

Consume the full JSON output. Never pipe through `head`, `tail`, `grep`, or `jq`. The output's `contextDir` field tells you where the files were resolved from.

If the output is already in this session's conversation history, don't re-run. Exceptions requiring a fresh load: you just ran `/impeccable teach` or `/impeccable document` (they rewrite the files), or the user manually edited one.

`/impeccable live` already warms context via `live.mjs` â€” if you've run `live.mjs`, don't also run `load-context.mjs` this session.

If PRODUCT.md is missing, empty, or placeholder (`[TODO]` markers, <200 chars): run `/impeccable teach`, then resume the user's original task with the fresh context. If the original task was `/impeccable craft`, resume into `/impeccable shape` before any implementation work.

If DESIGN.md is missing: nudge once per session (*"Run `/impeccable document` for more on-brand output"*), then proceed.

### 2. Register

Every design task is **brand** (marketing, landing, campaign, long-form content, portfolio â€” design IS the product) or **product** (app UI, admin, dashboard, tool â€” design SERVES the product).

Identify before designing. Priority: (1) cue in the task itself ("landing page" vs "dashboard"); (2) the surface in focus (the page, file, or route being worked on); (3) `register` field in PRODUCT.md. First match wins.

If PRODUCT.md lacks the `register` field (legacy), infer it once from its "Users" and "Product Purpose" sections, then cache the inferred value for the session. Suggest the user run `/impeccable teach` to add the field explicitly.

Load the matching reference: [reference/brand.md](reference/brand.md) or [reference/product.md](reference/product.md). The shared design laws below apply to both.

## Shared design laws

Apply to every design, both registers. Match implementation complexity to the aesthetic vision â€” maximalism needs elaborate code, minimalism needs precision. Interpret creatively. Vary across projects; never converge on the same choices. Codex is capable of extraordinary work â€” don't hold back.

### Color

- Use OKLCH. Reduce chroma as lightness approaches 0 or 100 â€” high chroma at extremes looks garish.
- Never use `#000` or `#fff`. Tint every neutral toward the brand hue (chroma 0.005â€“0.01 is enough).
- Pick a **color strategy** before picking colors. Four steps on the commitment axis:
  - **Restrained** â€” tinted neutrals + one accent â‰¤10%. Product default; brand minimalism.
  - **Committed** â€” one saturated color carries 30â€“60% of the surface. Brand default for identity-driven pages.
  - **Full palette** â€” 3â€“4 named roles, each used deliberately. Brand campaigns; product data viz.
  - **Drenched** â€” the surface IS the color. Brand heroes, campaign pages.
- The "one accent â‰¤10%" rule is Restrained only. Committed / Full palette / Drenched exceed it on purpose. Don't collapse every design to Restrained by reflex.

### Theme

Dark vs. light is never a default. Not dark "because tools look cool dark." Not light "to be safe."

Before choosing, write one sentence of physical scene: who uses this, where, under what ambient light, in what mood. If the sentence doesn't force the answer, it's not concrete enough â€” add detail until it does.

"Observability dashboard" does not force an answer. "SRE glancing at incident severity on a 27-inch monitor at 2am in a dim room" does. Run the sentence, not the category.

### Typography

- Cap body line length at 65â€“75ch.
- Hierarchy through scale + weight contrast (â‰¥1.25 ratio between steps). Avoid flat scales.

### Layout

- Vary spacing for rhythm. Same padding everywhere is monotony.
- Cards are the lazy answer. Use them only when they're truly the best affordance. Nested cards are always wrong.
- Don't wrap everything in a container. Most things don't need one.

### Motion

- Don't animate CSS layout properties.
- Ease out with exponential curves (ease-out-quart / quint / expo). No bounce, no elastic.

### Absolute bans

Match-and-refuse. If you're about to write any of these, rewrite the element with different structure.

- **Side-stripe borders.** `border-left` or `border-right` greater than 1px as a colored accent on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts. Never intentional. Rewrite with full borders, background tints, leading numbers/icons, or nothing.
- **Gradient text.** `background-clip: text` combined with a gradient background. Decorative, never meaningful. Use a single solid color. Emphasis via weight or size.
- **Glassmorphism as default.** Blurs and glass cards used decoratively. Rare and purposeful, or nothing.
- **The hero-metric template.** Big number, small label, supporting stats, gradient accent. SaaS clichÃ©.
- **Identical card grids.** Same-sized cards with icon + heading + text, repeated endlessly.
- **Modal as first thought.** Modals are usually laziness. Exhaust inline / progressive alternatives first.

### Copy

- Every word earns its place. No restated headings, no intros that repeat the title.
- **No em dashes.** Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses. Also not `--`.

### The AI slop test

If someone could look at this interface and say "AI made that" without doubt, it's failed. Cross-register failures are the absolute bans above. Register-specific failures live in each reference.

**Category-reflex check.** Run at two altitudes â€” the second one catches what the first one misses.

- **First-order:** if someone could guess the theme + palette from the category alone â€” "observability â†’ dark blue", "healthcare â†’ white + teal", "finance â†’ navy + gold", "crypto â†’ neon on black" â€” it's the first training-data reflex. Rework the scene sentence and color strategy until the answer isn't obvious from the domain.
- **Second-order:** if someone could guess the aesthetic family from category-plus-anti-references â€” "AI workflow tool that's not SaaS-cream â†’ editorial-typographic", "fintech that's not navy-and-gold â†’ terminal-native dark mode" â€” it's the trap one tier deeper. The first reflex was avoided; the second wasn't. Rework until both answers are not obvious. The brand register's [reflex-reject aesthetic lanes](reference/brand.md) list catches the currently-saturated families.

## Commands

| Command | Category | Description | Reference |
|---|---|---|---|
| `craft [feature]` | Build | Shape, then build a feature end-to-end | [reference/craft.md](reference/craft.md) |

| `harden [target]` | Refine | Production-ready: errors, i18n, edge cases | [reference/harden.md](reference/harden.md) |

| `optimize [target]` | Fix | Diagnose and fix UI performance | [reference/optimize.md](reference/optimize.md) |
| `live` | Iterate | Visual variant mode: pick elements in the browser, generate alternatives | [reference/live.md](reference/live.md) |

Plus two management commands â€” `pin <command>` and `unpin <command>`, detailed below.

### Routing rules

1. **No argument** â€” render the table above as the user-facing command menu, grouped by category. Ask what they'd like to do.
2. **First word matches a command** â€” load its reference file and follow its instructions. Everything after the command name is the target.
3. **First word doesn't match** â€” general design invocation. Apply the setup steps, shared design laws, and the loaded register reference, using the full argument as context.

Setup (context gathering, register) is already loaded by then; sub-commands don't re-invoke `/impeccable`.

If the first word is `craft`, setup still runs first, but [reference/craft.md](reference/craft.md) owns the rest of the flow. If setup invokes `teach` as a blocker, finish teach, refresh context, then resume the original command and target.

## Pin / Unpin

**Pin** creates a standalone shortcut so `/<command>` invokes `/impeccable <command>` directly. **Unpin** removes it. The script writes to every harness directory present in the project.

```bash
node .Codex/skills/impeccable/scripts/pin.mjs <pin|unpin> <command>
```

Valid `<command>` is any command from the table above. Report the script's result concisely â€” confirm the new shortcut on success, relay stderr verbatim on error.

### C:\Users\Kane(Don't delete C\.agents\skills\impeccable\reference\audit.md
Run systematic **technical** quality checks and generate a comprehensive report. Don't fix issues â€” document them for other commands to address.

This is a code-level audit, not a design critique. Check what's measurable and verifiable in the implementation.

## Diagnostic Scan

Run comprehensive checks across 5 dimensions. Score each dimension 0-4 using the criteria below.

### 1. Accessibility (A11y)

**Check for**:
- **Contrast issues**: Text contrast ratios < 4.5:1 (or 7:1 for AAA)
- **Missing ARIA**: Interactive elements without proper roles, labels, or states
- **Keyboard navigation**: Missing focus indicators, illogical tab order, keyboard traps
- **Semantic HTML**: Improper heading hierarchy, missing landmarks, divs instead of buttons
- **Alt text**: Missing or poor image descriptions
- **Form issues**: Inputs without labels, poor error messaging, missing required indicators

**Score 0-4**: 0=Inaccessible (fails WCAG A), 1=Major gaps (few ARIA labels, no keyboard nav), 2=Partial (some a11y effort, significant gaps), 3=Good (WCAG AA mostly met, minor gaps), 4=Excellent (WCAG AA fully met, approaches AAA)

### 2. Performance

**Check for**:
- **Layout thrashing**: Reading/writing layout properties in loops
- **Expensive animations**: Casual layout-property animation, unbounded blur/filter/shadow effects, or effects that visibly drop frames
- **Missing optimization**: Images without lazy loading, unoptimized assets, missing will-change
- **Bundle size**: Unnecessary imports, unused dependencies
- **Render performance**: Unnecessary re-renders, missing memoization

**Score 0-4**: 0=Severe issues (layout thrash, unoptimized everything), 1=Major problems (no lazy loading, expensive animations), 2=Partial (some optimization, gaps remain), 3=Good (mostly optimized, minor improvements possible), 4=Excellent (fast, lean, well-optimized)

### 3. Theming

**Check for**:
- **Hard-coded colors**: Colors not using design tokens
- **Broken dark mode**: Missing dark mode variants, poor contrast in dark theme
- **Inconsistent tokens**: Using wrong tokens, mixing token types
- **Theme switching issues**: Values that don't update on theme change

**Score 0-4**: 0=No theming (hard-coded everything), 1=Minimal tokens (mostly hard-coded), 2=Partial (tokens exist but inconsistently used), 3=Good (tokens used, minor hard-coded values), 4=Excellent (full token system, dark mode works perfectly)

### 4. Responsive Design

**Check for**:
- **Fixed widths**: Hard-coded widths that break on mobile
- **Touch targets**: Interactive elements < 44x44px
- **Horizontal scroll**: Content overflow on narrow viewports
- **Text scaling**: Layouts that break when text size increases
- **Missing breakpoints**: No mobile/tablet variants

**Score 0-4**: 0=Desktop-only (breaks on mobile), 1=Major issues (some breakpoints, many failures), 2=Partial (works on mobile, rough edges), 3=Good (responsive, minor touch target or overflow issues), 4=Excellent (fluid, all viewports, proper touch targets)

### 5. Anti-Patterns (CRITICAL)

Check against ALL the **DON'T** guidelines from the parent impeccable skill (already loaded in this context). Look for AI slop tells (AI color palette, gradient text, glassmorphism, hero metrics, card grids, generic fonts) and general design anti-patterns (gray on color, nested cards, bounce easing, redundant copy).

**Score 0-4**: 0=AI slop gallery (5+ tells), 1=Heavy AI aesthetic (3-4 tells), 2=Some tells (1-2 noticeable), 3=Mostly clean (subtle issues only), 4=No AI tells (distinctive, intentional design)

## Generate Report

### Audit Health Score

| # | Dimension | Score | Key Finding |
|---|-----------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | Accessibility | ? | [most critical a11y issue or "--"] |
| 2 | Performance | ? | |
| 3 | Responsive Design | ? | |
| 4 | Theming | ? | |
| 5 | Anti-Patterns | ? | |
| **Total** | | **??/20** | **[Rating band]** |

**Rating bands**: 18-20 Excellent (minor polish), 14-17 Good (address weak dimensions), 10-13 Acceptable (significant work needed), 6-9 Poor (major overhaul), 0-5 Critical (fundamental issues)

### Anti-Patterns Verdict
**Start here.** Pass/fail: Does this look AI-generated? List specific tells. Be brutally honest.

### Executive Summary
- Audit Health Score: **??/20** ([rating band])
- Total issues found (count by severity: P0/P1/P2/P3)
- Top 3-5 critical issues
- Recommended next steps

### Detailed Findings by Severity

Tag every issue with **P0-P3 severity**:
- **P0 Blocking**: Prevents task completion â€” fix immediately
- **P1 Major**: Significant difficulty or WCAG AA violation â€” fix before release
- **P2 Minor**: Annoyance, workaround exists â€” fix in next pass
- **P3 Polish**: Nice-to-fix, no real user impact â€” fix if time permits

For each issue, document:
- **[P?] Issue name**
- **Location**: Component, file, line
- **Category**: Accessibility / Performance / Theming / Responsive / Anti-Pattern
- **Impact**: How it affects users
- **WCAG/Standard**: Which standard it violates (if applicable)
- **Recommendation**: How to fix it
- **Suggested command**: Which command to use (prefer: /impeccable adapt, /impeccable animate, /impeccable audit, /impeccable bolder, /impeccable clarify, /impeccable colorize, /impeccable critique, /impeccable delight, /impeccable distill, /impeccable document, /impeccable harden, /impeccable layout, /impeccable onboard, /impeccable optimize, /impeccable overdrive, /impeccable polish, /impeccable quieter, /impeccable shape, /impeccable typeset)

### Patterns & Systemic Issues

Identify recurring problems that indicate systemic gaps rather than one-off mistakes:
- "Hard-coded colors appear in 15+ components, should use design tokens"
- "Touch targets consistently too small (<44px) throughout mobile experience"

### Positive Findings

Note what's working well â€” good practices to maintain and replicate.

## Recommended Actions

List recommended commands in priority order (P0 first, then P1, then P2):

1. **[P?] `/command-name`** â€” Brief description (specific context from audit findings)
2. **[P?] `/command-name`** â€” Brief description (specific context)

**Rules**: Only recommend commands from: /impeccable adapt, /impeccable animate, /impeccable audit, /impeccable bolder, /impeccable clarify, /impeccable colorize, /impeccable critique, /impeccable delight, /impeccable distill, /impeccable document, /impeccable harden, /impeccable layout, /impeccable onboard, /impeccable optimize, /impeccable overdrive, /impeccable polish, /impeccable quieter, /impeccable shape, /impeccable typeset. Map findings to the most appropriate command. End with `/impeccable polish` as the final step if any fixes were recommended.

After presenting the summary, tell the user:

> You can ask me to run these one at a time, all at once, or in any order you prefer.
>
> Re-run `/impeccable audit` after fixes to see your score improve.

**IMPORTANT**: Be thorough but actionable. Too many P3 issues creates noise. Focus on what actually matters.

**NEVER**:
- Report issues without explaining impact (why does this matter?)
- Provide generic recommendations (be specific and actionable)
- Skip positive findings (celebrate what works)
- Forget to prioritize (everything can't be P0)
- Report false positives without verification

Remember: You're a technical quality auditor. Document systematically, prioritize ruthlessly, cite specific code locations, and provide clear paths to improvement.

### C:\Users\Kane(Don't delete C\.agents\skills\impeccable\reference\product.md
# Product register

When design SERVES the product: app UIs, admin dashboards, settings panels, data tables, tools, authenticated surfaces, anything where the user is in a task.

## The product slop test

Not "would someone say AI made this" â€” familiarity is often a feature here. The test is: would a user fluent in the category's best tools (Linear, Figma, Notion, Raycast, Stripe come to mind) sit down and trust this interface, or pause at every subtly-off component?

Product UI's failure mode isn't flatness, it's strangeness without purpose: over-decorated buttons, mismatched form controls, gratuitous motion, display fonts where labels should be, invented affordances for standard tasks. The bar is earned familiarity. The tool should disappear into the task.

## Typography

- **System fonts are legitimate.** `-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif` gives you native feel on every platform. Inter is the common cross-platform default for a reason.
- **One family is often right.** Product UIs don't need display/body pairing. A well-tuned sans carries headings, buttons, labels, body, data.
- **Fixed rem scale, not fluid.** Clamp-sized headings don't serve product UI. Users view at consistent DPI, and a fluid h1 that shrinks in a sidebar looks worse, not better.
- **Tighter scale ratio.** 1.125â€“1.2 between steps is typical. More type elements here than on brand surfaces; exaggerated contrast creates noise.
- **Line length still applies for prose** (65â€“75ch). Data and compact UI can run denser â€” tables at 120ch+ are fine.

## Color

Product defaults to Restrained. A single surface can earn Committed â€” a dashboard where one category color carries a report, an onboarding flow with a drenched welcome screen â€” but Restrained is the floor.

- State-rich semantic vocabulary: hover, focus, active, disabled, selected, loading, error, warning, success, info. Standardize these.
- Accent color used for primary actions, current selection, and state indicators only â€” not decoration.
- A second neutral layer for sidebars, toolbars, and panels (slightly cooler or warmer than the content surface).

## Layout

- Predictable grids. Consistency IS an affordance â€” users navigate faster when the structure is expected.
- Familiar patterns are features. Standard navigation (top bar, side nav), breadcrumbs, tabs, and form layouts have established user expectations. Don't reinvent for flavor.
- Responsive behavior is structural (collapse sidebar, responsive table, breakpoint-driven columns), not fluid typography.

## Components

Every interactive component has: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error. Don't ship with half of these.

- Skeleton states for loading, not spinners in the middle of content.
- Empty states that teach the interface, not "nothing here."
- Consistent affordances across the surface. Same button shape. Same form-control vocabulary. Same icon style.

## Motion

- 150â€“250 ms on most transitions. Users are in flow â€” don't make them wait for choreography.
- Motion conveys state, not decoration. State change, feedback, loading, reveal â€” nothing else.
- No orchestrated page-load sequences. Product loads into a task; users don't want to watch it load.

## Product bans (on top of the shared absolute bans)

- Decorative motion that doesn't convey state.
- Inconsistent component vocabulary across screens. If the "save" button looks different in two places, one is wrong.
- Display fonts in UI labels, buttons, data.
- Reinventing standard affordances for flavor (custom scrollbars, weird form controls, non-standard modals).
- Heavy color or full-saturation accents on inactive states.

## Product permissions

Product can afford things brand surfaces can't.

- System fonts and familiar sans defaults (Inter, SF Pro, system-ui stacks).
- Standard navigation patterns: top bar + side nav, breadcrumbs, tabs, command palettes.
- Density. Tables with many rows, panels with many labels, dense information when users need it.
- Consistency over surprise. The same visual vocabulary screen to screen is a virtue; delight is saved for moments, not pages.

    2: 
    3: import { cn } from '@/lib/utils/cn'
    4: 
    5: export interface TabItem<T extends string = string> { id: T; label: string }
    6: interface TabsProps<T extends string> { items: readonly TabItem<T>[]; value: T; onValueChange: (value: T) => void; ariaLabel: string; className?: string }
    7: 
    8: export function Tabs<T extends string>({ items, value, onValueChange, ariaLabel, className }: TabsProps<T>) {
    9:   return (
   10:     <div className={cn('flex gap-1 border-b border-edge', className)} role="tablist" aria-label={ariaLabel}>
   11:       {items.map(item => <button key={item.id} type="button" role="tab" aria-selected={value === item.id} onClick={() => onValueChange(item.id)}
   12:         className={cn('border-b-2 px-3 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition', value === item.id ? 'border-accent text-accent' : 'border-transparent text-muted hover:text-primary')}>
   13:         {item.label}
   14:       </button>)}
   15:     </div>
   16:   )
   17: }

### src/components/shared/Modal.tsx
    1: 'use client'
    2: 
    3: import { X } from 'lucide-react'
    4: import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
    5: import { cn } from '@/lib/utils/cn'
    6: 
    7: interface ModalProps { open: boolean; onClose: () => void; title: string; children: ReactNode; footer?: ReactNode; className?: string }
    8: 
    9: export function Modal({ open, onClose, title, children, footer, className }: ModalProps) {
   10:   if (!open) return null
   11:   return (
   12:     <div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 grid place-items-center p-4" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="shared-modal-title">
   13:       <button className="absolute inset-0 bg-bg/80 backdrop-blur-sm" onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close dialog" />
   14:       <div className={cn('relative z-10 max-h-[90vh] w-full max-w-lg overflow-auto rounded-xl border border-edge bg-surface shadow-lg', className)}>
   15:         <header className="flex items-center justify-between border-b border-edge px-5 py-4">
   16:           <h2 id="shared-modal-title" className="font-semibold text-primary">{title}</h2>
   17:           <button onClick={onClose} className="grid h-8 w-8 place-items-center rounded-lg text-muted hover:bg-raised hover:text-primary" aria-label="Close"><X className="h-4 w-4" /></button>
   18:         </header>
   19:         <div className="p-5 text-secondary">{children}</div>
   20:         {footer && <footer className="flex justify-end gap-2 border-t border-edge bg-raised px-5 py-4">{footer}</footer>}
   21:       </div>
   22:     </div>
   23:   )
   24: }

   76:     { label: 'Delivery', href: '/settings/delivery' },
   77:   ]},
   78: ]
   79: 
   80: export default function Sidebar({ collapsed, onToggle }: { collapsed: boolean; onToggle: () => void }) {
   81:   const pathname = usePathname()
   82:   const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set())
   83: 
   84:   // Auto-expand active section
   85:   useEffect(() => {
   86:     void Promise.resolve().then(() => {
   87:       for (const item of navigation) {
   88:         if (item.children?.some(c => pathname?.startsWith(c.href))) {
   89:           setExpanded(prev => new Set(prev).add(item.label))
   90:         }
   91:       }
   92:     })
   93:   }, [pathname])
   94: 
   95:   const toggle = (label: string) => {
   96:     setExpanded(prev => {
   97:       const next = new Set(prev)
   98:       if (next.has(label)) next.delete(label); else next.add(label)
   99:       return next
  100:     })
  101:   }
  102: 
  103:   const isActive = (href: string) => pathname === href || pathname?.startsWith(href + '/')
  104: 
  105:   return (
  106:     <aside className={cn(
  107:       'bg-surface border-r border-edge flex flex-col shrink-0 transition-all duration-200 overflow-hidden',
  108:       collapsed ? 'w-16' : 'w-60',
  109:     )}>
  110:       <nav className="flex-1 py-2 overflow-y-auto">
  111:         {navigation.map((item) => {
  112:           const Icon = item.icon
  113:           const active = item.href ? isActive(item.href) : item.children?.some(c => isActive(c.href))
  114:           const isExpanded = expanded.has(item.label)
  115: 
  116:           if (item.href) {
  117:             return (
  118:               <Link key={item.label} href={item.href}
  119:                 className={cn('flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2 text-sm transition-colors',
  120:                   active ? 'bg-raised text-accent font-medium' : 'text-secondary hover:bg-raised/50 hover:text-primary')}>
  121:                 <Icon size={18} className="shrink-0" />
  122:                 {!collapsed && <span className="truncate">{item.label}</span>}
  123:               </Link>
  124:             )
  125:           }
  126: 
  127:           return (
  128:             <div key={item.label}>
  129:               <button onClick={() => toggle(item.label)}
  130:                 className={cn('w-full flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2 text-sm transition-colors',
  131:                   active ? 'text-accent' : 'text-secondary hover:bg-raised/50 hover:text-primary')}>
  132:                 <Icon size={18} className="shrink-0" />
  133:                 {!collapsed && (
  134:                   <>
  135:                     <span className="flex-1 text-left truncate">{item.label}</span>
  136:                     <ChevronDown size={14} className={cn('transition-transform', isExpanded && 'rotate-180')} />
  137:                   </>
  138:                 )}
  139:               </button>
  140:               {!collapsed && isExpanded && item.children && (
  141:                 <div className="ml-7 border-l border-edge">
  142:                   {item.children.map(child => (
  143:                     <Link key={child.href} href={child.href}
  144:                       className={cn('block pl-4 py-1.5 text-xs transition-colors',
  145:                         isActive(child.href) ? 'text-accent font-medium' : 'text-secondary hover:text-primary')}>
  146:                       {child.label}
  147:                     </Link>
  148:                   ))}
  149:                 </div>
  150:               )}
  151:             </div>
  152:           )
  153:         })}
  154:       </nav>
  155:     </aside>
  156:   )
  157: }

### src/components/backoffice/LocationSwitcher.tsx
    1: 'use client'
    2: 
    3: import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'
    4: import { useSelectedLocation } from '@/hooks/useSelectedLocation'
    5: 
    6: interface LocationOption {
    7:   id: string
    8:   name: string
    9:   city: string
   10:   state: string
   11: }
   12: 
   13: export default function LocationSwitcher() {
   14:   const { locationId, locationName, setLocation } = useSelectedLocation()
   15:   const [locations, setLocations] = useState<LocationOption[]>([])
   16:   const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
   17:   const [search, setSearch] = useState('')
   18: 
   19:   useEffect(() => {
   20:     fetch('/api/auth/locations').then(r => r.json()).then(d => setLocations(d.locations ?? []))
   21:   }, [])
   22: 
   23:   const filtered = search ? locations.filter(l => l.name.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase())) : locations
   24: 
   25:   return (
   26:     <div className="relative">
   27:       <button onClick={() => setOpen(!open)}
   28:         className="flex items-center gap-2 px-3 py-1.5 bg-raised rounded-lg text-sm text-primary hover:bg-raised max-w-[220px]">
   29:         <span className="truncate">{locationName}</span>
   30:         <span className="text-secondary text-xs shrink-0">â–¾</span>
   31:       </button>
   32: 
   33:       {open && (
   34:         <>
   35:           <div className="fixed inset-0 z-40" onClick={() => { setOpen(false); setSearch('') }} />
   36:           <div className="absolute right-0 top-full mt-1 w-72 bg-surface border border-edge rounded-lg shadow-xl z-50 overflow-hidden">
   37:             <div className="p-2 border-b border-edge">
   38:               <input value={search} onChange={e => setSearch(e.target.value)} placeholder="Search locations..."
   39:                 className="w-full h-8 px-2 bg-bg border border-edge-strong rounded text-sm text-primary focus:outline-none" autoFocus />
   40:             </div>
   41:             <div className="max-h-64 overflow-y-auto">
   42:               <button onClick={() => { setLocation(null); setOpen(false); setSearch('') }}
   43:                 className={`w-full text-left px-3 py-2 text-sm hover:bg-raised ${!locationId ? 'text-accent font-medium' : 'text-secondary'}`}>
   44:                 All Locations
   45:               </button>
   46:               {filtered.map(l => (
   47:                 <button key={l.id} onClick={() => { setLocation(l.id, l.name); setOpen(false); setSearch('') }}
   48:                   className={`w-full text-left px-3 py-2 text-sm hover:bg-raised ${locationId === l.id ? 'text-accent font-medium' : 'text-secondary'}`}>
   49:                   {l.name}
   50:                   <span className="text-xs text-muted ml-1">â€” {l.city}</span>
   51:                 </button>
   52:               ))}
   53:             </div>
   54:           </div>
   55:         </>
   56:       )}
   57:     </div>
   58:   )
   59: }

### src/components/terminal/CartSidebar.tsx
    1: 'use client'
    2: 
    3: import { useState } from 'react'
    4: import { useCart } from '@/hooks/useCart'
    5: import { useSession } from '@/hooks/useSession'
    6: import { useCashDrawer } from '@/hooks/useCashDrawer'
    7: import CustomerBadge from './CustomerBadge'
    8: import CustomerSearchPanel from './CustomerSearchPanel'
    9: import QuickCustomerForm from './QuickCustomerForm'
   10: import CustomerProfileDrawer from './CustomerProfileDrawer'
   11: import PurchaseLimitGauge from './PurchaseLimitGauge'
   12: import CheckoutPanel from './CheckoutPanel'
   13: 
   14: function fmt(n: number) {
   15:   return n.toLocaleString('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' })
   16: }
   17: 
   18: export default function CartSidebar() {
   19:   const {
   20:     items, customerId, customerName, subtotal, discountTotal, taxTotal, total,
   21:     removeItem, updateQuantity, clearCart, purchaseLimit, discountResult,
   22:     heldCarts, holdCart, resumeCart, deleteHeldCart,
   23:   } = useCart()
   24:   const { session } = useSession()
   25:   const { drawer, openDrawer } = useCashDrawer(session?.registerId ?? '')
   26:   const [showSearch, setShowSearch] = useState(false)
   27:   const [showOpenDrawer, setShowOpenDrawer] = useState(false)
   28:   const [drawerAmount, setDrawerAmount] = useState('200')
   29:   const [showNewCustomer, setShowNewCustomer] = useState(false)
   30:   const [showProfile, setShowProfile] = useState(false)
   31:   const [showCheckout, setShowCheckout] = useState(false)
   32:   const [showClearConfirm, setShowClearConfirm] = useState(false)
   33:   const [showHeldCarts, setShowHeldCarts] = useState(false)
   34:   const [toast, setToast] = useState<string | null>(null)
   35:   const [now] = useState(() => Date.now())
   36: 
   37:   const canPay = items.length > 0 && (purchaseLimit?.allowed ?? true) && !!drawer
   38: 
   39:   const handleVoidClick = () => {
   40:     if (items.length === 0) return
   41:     setShowClearConfirm(true)
   42:   }
   43: 
   44:   const confirmClear = () => {
   45:     clearCart()
   46:     setShowClearConfirm(false)
   47:   }
   48: 
   49:   const handleHoldClick = () => {
   50:     if (items.length === 0) return
   51:     if (heldCarts.length >= 10) return
   52:     holdCart(session?.employeeName ?? 'Unknown')
   53:     setToast('Sale held')
   54:     setTimeout(() => setToast(null), 2000)
   55:   }
   56: 
   57:   const handleResume = (id: string) => {
   58:     if (items.length > 0) {
   59:       holdCart(session?.employeeName ?? 'Unknown')
   60:     }
   61:     resumeCart(id)
   62:     setShowHeldCarts(false)
   63:   }
   64: 
   65:   const getHeldAge = (heldAt: string) => {
   66:     const mins = Math.floor((now - new Date(heldAt).getTime()) / 60000)
   67:     if (mins < 1) return '<1m'
   68:     if (mins < 60) return `${mins}m`
   69:     return `${Math.floor(mins / 60)}h ${mins % 60}m`
   70:   }
   71: 
   72:   return (
   73:     <aside className="w-[420px] shrink-0 flex flex-col bg-bg border-l border-edge relative">
   74:       {/* Modals & Panels */}
   75:       {showSearch && (
   76:         <CustomerSearchPanel
   77:           onClose={() => setShowSearch(false)}
   78:           onNewCustomer={() => { setShowSearch(false); setShowNewCustomer(true) }}
   79:         />
   80:       )}
   81:       {showNewCustomer && <QuickCustomerForm onClose={() => setShowNewCustomer(false)} />}
   82:       {showProfile && customerId && (
   83:         <CustomerProfileDrawer customerId={customerId} onClose={() => setShowProfile(false)} />
   84:       )}
   85:       {showCheckout && (
   86:         <CheckoutPanel onClose={() => setShowCheckout(false)} cashDrawerId={drawer?.id ?? ''} />
   87:       )}
   88: 
   89:       {/* Clear Confirm Dialog */}
   90:       {showClearConfirm && (
   91:         <div className="fixed inset-0 bg-bg/60 z-50 flex items-center justify-center">
   92:           <div className="bg-surface border border-edge rounded-2xl w-full max-w-sm p-6">
   93:             <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-primary mb-2">Clear this sale?</h3>
   94:             <p className="text-sm text-secondary mb-6">All items will be removed from the cart.</p>
   95:             <div className="flex gap-2 justify-end">
   96:               <button onClick={() => setShowClearConfirm(false)} className="px-4 py-2 bg-raised text-secondary rounded-lg text-sm">Cancel</button>
   97:               <button onClick={confirmClear} className="px-4 py-2 bg-danger text-primary rounded-lg text-sm hover:bg-danger">Clear Sale</button>
   98:             </div>
   99:           </div>
  100:         </div>
  101:       )}
  102: 
  103:       {/* Held Carts Panel */}
  104:       {showHeldCarts && (
  105:         <div className="fixed inset-0 bg-bg/60 z-50 flex items-center justify-center">
  106:           <div className="bg-surface border border-edge rounded-2xl w-full max-w-md p-6">
  107:             <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4">
  108:               <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-primary">Held Sales ({heldCarts.length})</h3>
  109:               <button onClick={() => setShowHeldCarts(false)} className="text-secondary hover:text-primary">&#10005;</button>
  110:             </div>
  111:             {heldCarts.length === 0 ? (
  112:               <p className="text-sm text-muted text-center py-4">No held sales</p>
  113:             ) : (
  114:               <ul className="space-y-2 max-h-80 overflow-y-auto">
  115:                 {heldCarts.map((held) => {
  116:                   const age = getHeldAge(held.heldAt)
  117:                   const isOld = (now - new Date(held.heldAt).getTime()) > 120 * 60000
  118:                   return (
  119:                     <li key={held.id} className={`bg-bg rounded-lg p-3 ${isOld ? 'border border-warning' : 'border border-edge'}`}>
  120:                       <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-1">
  121:                         <span className="text-sm text-primary">{held.customerName ?? 'Walk-in'}</span>
  122:                         <span className={`text-xs ${isOld ? 'text-warning' : 'text-muted'}`}>{age} ago</span>
  123:                       </div>
  124:                       <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-2">
  125:                         <span className="text-xs text-secondary">{held.itemCount} items &middot; {held.heldBy}</span>
  126:                         <span className="text-sm text-primary font-medium tabular-nums">{fmt(held.total)}</span>
  127:                       </div>
  128:                       <div className="flex gap-2">
  129:                         <button onClick={() => handleResume(held.id)} className="flex-1 h-8 bg-accent text-primary text-xs rounded-lg hover:bg-accent">Resume</button>
  130:                         <button onClick={() => deleteHeldCart(held.id)} className="h-8 px-3 bg-raised text-secondary text-xs rounded-lg hover:text-danger">Delete</button>
  131:                       </div>
  132:                     </li>
  133:                   )
  134:                 })}
  135:               </ul>
  136:             )}
  137:           </div>
  138:         </div>
  139:       )}
  140: 
  141:       {/* Open Drawer Dialog */}
  142:       {showOpenDrawer && (
  143:         <div className="fixed inset-0 bg-bg/60 z-50 flex items-center justify-center">
  144:           <div className="bg-surface border border-edge rounded-2xl w-full max-w-sm p-6">
  145:             <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-primary mb-2">Open Cash Drawer</h3>
  146:             <p className="text-sm text-secondary mb-4">Enter the opening amount to start selling.</p>
  147:             <label className="block mb-4">
  148:               <span className="text-xs text-secondary">Opening Amount ($)</span>
  149:               <input
  150:                 type="number" step="0.01" value={drawerAmount}
  151:                 onChange={e => setDrawerAmount(e.target.value)}
  152:                 className="w-full h-12 px-3 bg-bg border border-edge-strong rounded-lg text-primary text-lg text-center tabular-nums focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent"
  153:               />
  154:             </label>
  155:             <div className="flex gap-2 justify-end">
  156:               <button onClick={() => setShowOpenDrawer(false)} className="px-4 py-2 bg-raised text-secondary rounded-lg text-sm">Cancel</button>
  157:               <button
  158:                 onClick={async () => {
  159:                   if (session) {
  160:                     await openDrawer(parseFloat(drawerAmount) || 200, session.locationId, session.employeeId)
  161:                   }

   22: interface BarcodeScanResult {
   23:   product: {
   24:     id: string
   25:     name: string
   26:     sku: string | null
   27:     barcode: string | null
   28:     rec_price: number
   29:     med_price: number | null

   35:     strain_name: string | null

   46: interface ProductSearchProps {

   53: const QUICK_CATEGORIES = ['All', 'Flower', 'Concentrates', 'Edibles', 'Vapes', 'Pre-Rolls', 'Tinctures', 'Topicals', 'Accessories']
   54: 
   55: function playBeep() {
   56:   try {
   57:     const ctx = new AudioContext()
   58:     const osc = ctx.createOscillator()
   59:     const gain = ctx.createGain()
   60:     osc.connect(gain)
   61:     gain.connect(ctx.destination)
   62:     osc.frequency.value = 800
   63:     gain.gain.value = 0.15
   64:     osc.start()
   65:     osc.stop(ctx.currentTime + 0.15)
   66:     setTimeout(() => ctx.close(), 300)
   67:   } catch {
   68:     // Web Audio not available
   69:   }
   70: }
   71: 
   72: function StockBar({ quantity }: { quantity: number }) {
   73:   const max = 50
   74:   const pct = Math.min((quantity / max) * 100, 100)
   75:   const color =
   76:     quantity === 0
   77:       ? 'bg-danger'
   78:       : quantity <= 10
   79:         ? 'bg-warning'
   80:         : 'bg-accent'
   81:   const trackColor =
   82:     quantity === 0
   83:       ? 'bg-danger/20'
   84:       : quantity <= 10
   85:         ? 'bg-warning/20'
   86:         : 'bg-accent/20'
   87: 
   88:   return (
   89:     <div className={`w-full h-0.5 rounded-full ${trackColor}`}>
   90:       <div
   91:         className={`h-full rounded-full ${color} transition-all`}
   92:         style={{ width: `${pct}%` }}
   93:       />
   94:     </div>
   95:   )
   96: }
   97: 
   98: function SkeletonCard() {
   99:   return (
  100:     <div className="bg-bg border border-edge rounded-xl p-3 animate-pulse">
  101:       <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-1.5">
  102:         <div className="h-3 w-16 bg-surface rounded" />
  103:         <div className="h-4 w-12 bg-surface rounded" />
  104:       </div>
  105:       <div className="h-4 w-full bg-surface rounded mb-1" />
  106:       <div className="h-3 w-3/4 bg-surface rounded mb-2.5" />
  107:       <div className="h-3 w-2/3 bg-surface rounded mb-2" />
  108:       <div className="h-0.5 w-full bg-surface rounded" />
  109:     </div>
  110:   )
  111: }
  112: 
  113: export default function ProductSearch({ onSelect, onBarcodeScan, locationId, categoryId }: ProductSearchProps) {
  114:   const [query, setQuery] = useState('')
  115:   const [results, setResults] = useState<SearchResult[]>([])
  116:   const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false)
  117:   const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false)
  118:   const [isScanning, setIsScanning] = useState(false)
  119:   const [toast, setToast] = useState<{ message: string; type: 'success' | 'error' } | null>(null)
  120:   const [selectedIndex, setSelectedIndex] = useState(-1)
  121:   const [categoryFilter, setCategoryFilter] = useState('All')
  122:   const inputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)
  123:   const timerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(null)
  124:   const toastTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(null)
  125: 
  126:   useEffect(() => {
  127:     inputRef.current?.focus()
  128:   }, [])
  129: 
  130:   // Auto-browse when category changes
  131:   useEffect(() => {
  132:     if (categoryId) {
  133:       search(query, categoryId)
  134:     } else if (query.length < 2) {
  135:       setResults([])
  136:       setIsOpen(false)
  137:     }
  138:   // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
  139:   }, [categoryId])
  140: 
  141:   const showToast = (message: string, type: 'success' | 'error') => {
  142:     setToast({ message, type })
  143:     if (toastTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(toastTimerRef.current)
  144:     toastTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setToast(null), 2500)
  145:   }
  146: 
  147:   const scanBarcode = useCallback(async (code: string) => {
  148:     setIsScanning(true)
  149:     setIsLoading(true)
  150:     try {
  151:       const locParam = locationId ? `?location_id=${locationId}` : ''
  152:       const res = await fetch(`/api/products/barcode/${encodeURIComponent(code)}${locParam}`)
  153:       if (res.ok) {
  154:         const data: BarcodeScanResult = await res.json()
  155:         playBeep()
  156:         if (onBarcodeScan) {
  157:           onBarcodeScan(data)
  158:         }
  159:         showToast(`Added: ${data.product.name}`, 'success')
  160:         setQuery('')
  161:         setResults([])
  162:         setIsOpen(false)
  163:       } else {
  164:         showToast(`Barcode ${code} not found`, 'error')
  165:       }
  166:     } catch {
  167:       showToast('Scan failed â€” connection error', 'error')
  168:     } finally {
  169:       setIsLoading(false)
  170:       setIsScanning(false)
  171:       inputRef.current?.focus()
  172:     }
  173:   }, [locationId, onBarcodeScan])
  174: 
  175:   const abortRef = useRef<AbortController | null>(null)
  176: 
  177:   const search = useCallback(async (q: string, catId?: string) => {
  178:     if (q.length < 2 && !catId) {
  179:       setResults([])
  180:       setIsOpen(false)
  181:       return
  182:     }
  183:     // Cancel previous request
  184:     if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort()
  185:     const controller = new AbortController()
  186:     abortRef.current = controller
  187: 
  188:     setIsLoading(true)
  189:     try {
  190:       const params = new URLSearchParams()
  191:       if (q.length >= 2) params.set('query', q)
  192:       if (catId) params.set('category_id', catId)
  193:       const res = await fetch(`/api/products/search?${params}`, { signal: controller.signal })
  194:       if (res.ok) {
  195:         const data = await res.json()
  196:         setResults(data.results ?? [])
  197:         setIsOpen(true)
  198:       }
  199:     } catch (err) {
  200:       if (err instanceof DOMException && err.name === 'AbortError') return
  201:     } finally {
  202:       setIsLoading(false)
  203:     }
  204:   }, [])
  205: 
  206:   const handleChange = (value: string) => {
  207:     setQuery(value)
  208:     setSelectedIndex(-1)
  209:     if (timerRef.current) clearTimeout(timerRef.current)
  210:     timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => search(value, categoryId), 300)
  211:   }
  212: 
  213:   const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
  214:     if (e.key === 'Escape') {
  215:       setQuery('')
  216:       setResults([])
  217:       setIsOpen(false)
  218:       return
  219:     }
  220: 
  221:     if (e.key === 'Enter') {
  222:       e.preventDefault()
  223: 
  224:       // If grid result selected, use it
  225:       if (selectedIndex >= 0 && filteredResults[selectedIndex]) {
  226:         selectResult(filteredResults[selectedIndex])
  227:         return
  228:       }
  229: 
  230:       // Check if input looks like a barcode
  231:       if (isBarcodeInput(query.trim())) {
  232:         if (timerRef.current) clearTimeout(timerRef.current)
  233:         scanBarcode(query.trim())
  234:         return
  235:       }
  236: 
  237:       // Otherwise do text search
  238:       if (query.length >= 2) {
  239:         if (timerRef.current) clearTimeout(timerRef.current)
  240:         search(query)
  241:       }
  242:       return
  243:     }
  244: 
  245:     if (e.key === 'ArrowDown') {
  246:       e.preventDefault()
  247:       setSelectedIndex((i) => Math.min(i + 1, filteredResults.length - 1))
  248:     } else if (e.key === 'ArrowUp') {
  249:       e.preventDefault()
  250:       setSelectedIndex((i) => Math.max(i - 1, 0))
  251:     }
  252:   }
  253: 
  254:   const selectResult = (product: SearchResult) => {
  255:     onSelect(product)
  256:     setQuery('')
  257:     setResults([])
  258:     setIsOpen(false)
  259:     setSelectedIndex(-1)
  260:     inputRef.current?.focus()
  261:   }
  262: 
  263:   // Client-side category filtering
  264:   const filteredResults = categoryFilter === 'All'
  265:     ? results
  266:     : results.filter((r) => {
  267:         const cat = (r.category_name ?? '').toLowerCase()
  268:         const filter = categoryFilter.toLowerCase()
  269:         // Match category_name containing the filter word (e.g. "Flower" matches "Flower", "Pre-Rolls" matches "Pre-Roll")
  270:         return cat.includes(filter.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/s$/, ''))
  271:       })
  272: 
  273:   return (
  274:     <div className="flex flex-col gap-3">
  275:       {/* Toast */}
  276:       {toast && (
  277:         <div
  278:           className={`fixed bottom-24 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 px-4 py-2 rounded-lg text-sm font-medium z-50 shadow-xl border transition-opacity ${
  279:             toast.type === 'success'
  280:               ? 'bg-surface border-accent/30 text-primary'
  281:               : 'bg-surface border-danger/30 text-primary'
  282:           }`}
  283:         >
  284:           {toast.message}
  285:         </div>
  286:       )}
  287: 
  288:       {/* Search Input */}
  289:       <div className="relative">
  290:         <input
  291:           ref={inputRef}
  292:           type="text"
  293:           value={query}
  294:           onChange={(e) => handleChange(e.target.value)}
  295:           onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
  296:           onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setIsOpen(true)}
  297:           placeholder="Search products, scan barcode..."

  301:           <div className="absolute right-4 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2">

  303:           </div>

  306:             className="absolute right-4 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-5 h-5 text-accent pointer-events-none"

    6: function fmt(n: number) {
    7:   return n.toLocaleString('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' })
    8: }
    9: 
   10: const QUICK_AMOUNTS = [1, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100]
   11: 
   12: interface CheckoutPanelProps {
   13:   onClose: () => void
   14:   cashDrawerId: string
   15: }
   16: 
   17: export default function CheckoutPanel({ onClose, cashDrawerId }: CheckoutPanelProps) {
   18:   const {
   19:     items, customerId, customerType, subtotal, discountTotal, taxTotal, total, locationId, registerId,
   20:     manualDiscountIds, clearCart,
   21:   } = useCart()
   22: 
   23:   const [tendered, setTendered] = useState('')
   24:   const [isProcessing, setIsProcessing] = useState(false)
   25:   const [result, setResult] = useState<{ transactionNumber: number; changeDue: number } | null>(null)
   26:   const [error, setError] = useState('')
   27: 
   28:   const tenderedAmount = parseFloat(tendered) || 0
   29:   const changeDue = Math.max(0, Math.round((tenderedAmount - total) * 100) / 100)
   30:   const canComplete = tenderedAmount >= total && !isProcessing
   31: 
   32:   const handleExact = () => setTendered(total.toFixed(2))
   33: 
   34:   const handleQuick = (amount: number) => {
   35:     const current = parseFloat(tendered) || 0
   36:     setTendered((current + amount).toFixed(2))
   37:   }
   38: 
   39:   const handleComplete = useCallback(async () => {
   40:     setIsProcessing(true)
   41:     setError('')
   42: 
   43:     try {
   44:       const res = await fetch('/api/transactions', {
   45:         method: 'POST',
   46:         headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   47:         body: JSON.stringify({
   48:           location_id: locationId,
   49:           register_id: registerId || undefined,
   50:           cash_drawer_id: cashDrawerId,
   51:           customer_id: customerId,
   52:           is_medical: customerType === 'medical',
   53:           items: items.map((i) => ({
   54:             product_id: i.productId,
   55:             inventory_item_id: i.inventoryItemId,
   56:             quantity: i.quantity,
   57:           })),
   58:           amount_tendered: tenderedAmount,
   59:           payment_method: 'cash',
   60:           manual_discount_ids: manualDiscountIds,
   61:         }),
   62:       })
   63: 
   64:       const data = await res.json()
   65: 
   66:       if (!res.ok) {
   67:         setError(data.error ?? 'Transaction failed')
   68:         setIsProcessing(false)
   69:         return
   70:       }
   71: 
   72:       setResult({ transactionNumber: data.transactionNumber, changeDue: data.changeDue })
   73:     } catch {
   74:       setError('Connection error')
   75:       setIsProcessing(false)
   76:     }
   77:   }, [items, customerId, customerType, locationId, registerId, cashDrawerId, tenderedAmount, manualDiscountIds, clearCart, onClose])
   78: 
   79:   // Success screen
   80:   if (result) {
   81:     return <SuccessScreen result={result} clearCart={clearCart} onClose={onClose} />
   82:   }
   83: 
   84:   return (
   85:     <div className="fixed inset-0 bg-bg/90 backdrop-blur-sm z-50 flex items-center justify-center">
   86:       <div className="bg-bg border border-edge rounded-2xl w-full max-w-lg shadow-2xl">
   87:         {/* Header */}
   88:         <div className="px-6 pt-6 pb-4 border-b border-edge flex items-center justify-between">
   89:           <h2 className="text-lg font-bold text-primary">Complete Sale</h2>
   90:           <button
   91:             onClick={onClose}
   92:             className="w-8 h-8 rounded-lg hover:bg-surface/5 flex items-center justify-center text-muted hover:text-secondary transition-colors"
   93:           >
   94:             <svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round">
   95:               <path d="M4 4l8 8M12 4l-8 8" />
   96:             </svg>
   97:           </button>
   98:         </div>
   99: 
  100:         {/* Item count + customer */}
  101:         <p className="text-sm text-muted px-6 pt-3">
  102:           {items.length} item{items.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''}
  103:           {customerId ? ' \u00b7 Customer attached' : ''}
  104:         </p>
  105: 
  106:         {/* Total Due */}
  107:       <div className="mx-6 mt-4 bg-bg rounded-xl p-6 text-center">

  124:             placeholder="0.00"
  125:           />
  126:         </div>

  135:             >
  136:               ${amt}

  177: /* â”€â”€â”€ Success Screen â”€â”€â”€ */
  178: 
  179: function SuccessScreen({
  180:   result,
  181:   clearCart,
  182:   onClose,
  183: }: {
  184:   result: { transactionNumber: number; changeDue: number }
  185:   clearCart: () => void
  186:   onClose: () => void
  187: }) {
  188:   const [countdown, setCountdown] = useState(5)
  189:   const closedRef = useRef(false)
  190: 
  191:   useEffect(() => {
  192:     const id = setInterval(() => {
  193:       setCountdown((prev) => {
  194:         if (prev <= 1 && !closedRef.current) {
  195:           closedRef.current = true
  196:           clearCart()
  197:           onClose()
  198:         }
  199:         return prev - 1
  200:       })
  201:     }, 1000)
  202:     return () => clearInterval(id)
  203:   }, [clearCart, onClose])
  204: 
  205:   const handleNewSale = () => {
  206:     if (!closedRef.current) {
  207:       closedRef.current = true
  208:       clearCart()
  209:       onClose()
  210:     }
  211:   }
  212: 
  213:   return (
  214:     <div className="fixed inset-0 bg-bg/90 backdrop-blur-sm z-50 flex items-center justify-center">
  215:       <div className="bg-bg border border-edge rounded-2xl w-full max-w-lg shadow-2xl py-10 px-8 text-center">
  216:         {/* Checkmark */}
  217:         <svg
  218:           className="w-16 h-16 text-accent mx-auto"
  219:           viewBox="0 0 64 64"
  220:           fill="none"
  221:           stroke="currentColor"
  222:           strokeWidth="3"
  223:           strokeLinecap="round"
  224:           strokeLinejoin="round"
  225:         >
  226:           <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="28" className="text-accent/20" />
  227:           <path d="M20 33l8 8 16-16" />
  228:         </svg>
  229: 
  230:         <p className="text-lg text-secondary font-medium mt-4">Sale Complete</p>
  231:         <p className="text-sm text-muted font-mono mt-1">
  232:           Transaction #{result.transactionNumber}
  233:         </p>
  234: 
  235:         {/* Change Due */}
  236:         {result.changeDue > 0 && (
  237:           <div className="mt-6">
  238:             <p className="text-xs text-muted uppercase tracking-widest">Change Due</p>
  239:             <p className="text-5xl font-bold text-accent font-mono tabular-nums mt-1">
  240:               {fmt(result.changeDue)}
  241:             </p>
  242:           </div>
  243:         )}
  244: 
  245:         {/* New Sale */}
  246:         <button
  247:           onClick={handleNewSale}
  248:           className="mt-8 h-12 w-full max-w-xs mx-auto rounded-xl bg-surface border border-edge text-primary font-medium hover:bg-raised transition-colors block"
  249:         >
  250:           New Sale
  251:         </button>
  252: 
  253:         {/* Countdown */}

  256:         </p>
  257:       </div>
  258:     </div>
  259:   )

    1: 'use client'
    2: 
    3: import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'

   13: import { useBarcodeScanner } from '@/hooks/useBarcodeScanner'

   15: 
   16: function makeCartInput(p: {
   17:   id: string; name: string; sku?: string | null; rec_price: number;
   18:   is_cannabis: boolean; weight_grams?: number | null; flower_equivalent?: number | null;
   19:   brand_name?: string | null; category_name?: string | null; strain_name?: string | null;
   20: }, invId: string | null = null, barcode: string | null = null): CartItemInput {
   21:   return {
   22:     productId: p.id, inventoryItemId: invId, productName: p.name,
   23:     categoryId: '', categoryName: p.category_name ?? null,
   24:     brandId: null, brandName: p.brand_name ?? null,
   25:     vendorId: null, strainId: null, strainName: p.strain_name ?? null,
   26:     sku: p.sku ?? null, quantity: 1, unitPrice: p.rec_price,
   27:     isCannabis: p.is_cannabis, isMedical: false,
   28:     weightGrams: p.weight_grams ?? null, flowerEquivalent: p.flower_equivalent ?? null,
   29:     thcMg: null, biotrackBarcode: barcode, purchaseLimitCategory: null,
   30:     productTagIds: [], inventoryTagIds: [], pricingTierId: null, weightDescriptor: null,
   31:   }
   32: }
   33: 
   34: export default function CheckoutPage() {
   35:   const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<TerminalTab>('sale')
   36:   const [showNewCustomer, setShowNewCustomer] = useState(false)
   37:   const [selectedCategory, setSelectedCategory] = useState<{ id: string; name: string } | null>(null)
   38:   const addItem = useCart((s) => s.addItem)
   39:   const initializeCart = useCart((s) => s.initializeCart)
   40:   const configLoaded = useCart((s) => s.configLoaded)
   41:   const { session } = useSession()
   42: 
   43:   useEffect(() => {
   44:     if (session && !configLoaded) {
   45:       initializeCart({ locationId: session.locationId, organizationId: session.organizationId, employeeId: session.employeeId, registerId: session.registerId ?? '' })
   46:     }
   47:   }, [session, configLoaded, initializeCart])
   48: 
   49:   const handleBarcodeScan = useCallback(async (barcode: string) => {
   50:     if (!session) return
   51:     try {
   52:       const res = await fetch(`/api/products/barcode/${encodeURIComponent(barcode)}?location_id=${session.locationId}`)
   53:       if (res.ok) { const data = await res.json(); addItem(makeCartInput(data.product, data.inventory_item.id, data.inventory_item.biotrack_barcode)) }
   54:     } catch { /* toast handles */ }
   55:   }, [session, addItem])
   56: 
   57:   useBarcodeScanner({ onScan: handleBarcodeScan, enabled: !!session })
   58: 
   59:   return (
   60:     <TerminalLayout>
   61:       <div className="flex flex-col h-full min-h-0">
   62:         {/* Tab bar â€” fixed at top of left panel */}
   63:         <TerminalTabBar activeTab={activeTab} onTabChange={setActiveTab} />
   64: 
   65:         {/* Persistent search bar â€” Sale tab only */}
   66:         {activeTab === 'sale' && (
   67:           <div className="shrink-0 px-4 pt-3 pb-2 bg-bg border-b border-edge/50">
   68:             <ProductSearch
   69:               locationId={session?.locationId}
   70:               categoryId={selectedCategory?.id}
   71:               onSelect={(product) => addItem(makeCartInput(product))}
   72:               onBarcodeScan={(result) => addItem(makeCartInput(result.product, result.inventory_item.id, result.inventory_item.biotrack_barcode))}
   73:             />
   74:             {selectedCategory && (
   75:               <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-2">
   76:                 <div className="flex items-center gap-2 px-3 py-1 bg-accent/50 border border-accent/60 rounded-full text-xs text-accent font-medium">
   77:                   {selectedCategory.name}
   78:                   <button
   79:                     onClick={() => setSelectedCategory(null)}
   80:                     className="hover:text-primary transition-colors"
   81:                   >
   82:                     &#10005;

    2:   { id: 'oasis-dark', label: 'Oasis Dark' },
    3:   { id: 'oasis-light', label: 'Oasis Light' },

   10: export const THEME_STORAGE_KEY = 'oasis-theme'
   11: export const THEME_COOKIE_NAME = 'oasis-theme'
   12: 
   13: export function isThemeId(value: unknown): value is ThemeId {
   14:   return typeof value === 'string' && THEMES.some(theme => theme.id === value)

### src/lib/theme/ThemeProvider.tsx
    1: 'use client'
    2: 
    3: import { createContext, useCallback, useContext, useSyncExternalStore, type ReactNode } from 'react'
    4: import {
    5:   DEFAULT_THEME,
    6:   isThemeId,
    7:   THEME_COOKIE_NAME,
    8:   THEME_STORAGE_KEY,
    9:   type ThemeId,
   10: } from './registry'
   11: 
   12: const THEME_CHANGE_EVENT = 'oasis-theme-change'
   13: 
   14: function readTheme(): ThemeId {
   15:   if (typeof document === 'undefined') return DEFAULT_THEME
   16:   const value = document.documentElement.dataset.theme
   17:   return isThemeId(value) ? value : DEFAULT_THEME
   18: }
   19: 
   20: function subscribe(onStoreChange: () => void): () => void {
   21:   const handleStorage = (event: StorageEvent) => {
   22:     if (event.key !== THEME_STORAGE_KEY || !isThemeId(event.newValue)) return
   23:     document.documentElement.dataset.theme = event.newValue
   24:     onStoreChange()
   25:   }
   26:   window.addEventListener(THEME_CHANGE_EVENT, onStoreChange)
   27:   window.addEventListener('storage', handleStorage)
   28:   return () => {
   29:     window.removeEventListener(THEME_CHANGE_EVENT, onStoreChange)
   30:     window.removeEventListener('storage', handleStorage)
   31:   }
   32: }
   33: 
   34: interface ThemeContextValue {
   35:   theme: ThemeId
   36:   setTheme: (theme: ThemeId) => void
   37: }
   38: 
   39: const ThemeContext = createContext<ThemeContextValue | null>(null)
   40: 
   41: export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
   42:   const theme = useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, readTheme, () => DEFAULT_THEME)
   43: 
   44:   const setTheme = useCallback((nextTheme: ThemeId) => {
   45:     if (!isThemeId(nextTheme)) return
   46:     document.documentElement.dataset.theme = nextTheme
   47:     try {
   48:       localStorage.setItem(THEME_STORAGE_KEY, nextTheme)
   49:     } catch {
   50:       // Cookie persistence still works when storage access is blocked.
   51:     }
   52:     document.cookie = `${THEME_COOKIE_NAME}=${encodeURIComponent(nextTheme)}; Path=/; Max-Age=31536000; SameSite=Lax`
   53:     window.dispatchEvent(new Event(THEME_CHANGE_EVENT))
   54:   }, [])
   55: 
   56:   return <ThemeContext.Provider value={{ theme, setTheme }}>{children}</ThemeContext.Provider>
   57: }
   58: 
   59: export function useTheme(): ThemeContextValue {
   60:   const value = useContext(ThemeContext)
   61:   if (!value) throw new Error('useTheme must be used within ThemeProvider')
   62:   return value
   63: }

'use client'

import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'
import { useSelectedLocation } from '@/hooks/useSelectedLocation'

interface LocationOption {
  id: string
  name: string
  city: string
  state: string
}

export default function LocationSwitcher() {
  const { locationId, locationName, setLocation } = useSelectedLocation()
  const [locations, setLocations] = useState<LocationOption[]>([])
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
  const [search, setSearch] = useState('')

  useEffect(() => {
    fetch('/api/auth/locations').then(r => r.json()).then(d => setLocations(d.locations ?? []))
  }, [])

  const filtered = search ? locations.filter(l => l.name.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase())) : locations

  return (
    <div className="relative">
      <button onClick={() => setOpen(!open)}
        className="flex items-center gap-2 px-3 py-1.5 [-bg-gray-700-]{+bg-raised+} rounded-lg text-sm [-text-gray-200 hover:bg-gray-600-]{+text-primary hover:bg-raised+} max-w-[220px]">
        <span className="truncate">{locationName}</span>
        <span [-className="text-gray-400-]{+className="text-secondary+} text-xs shrink-0">▾</span>
      </button>

      {open && (
        <>
          <div className="fixed inset-0 z-40" onClick={() => { setOpen(false); setSearch('') }} />
          <div className="absolute right-0 top-full mt-1 w-72 [-bg-gray-800-]{+bg-surface+} border [-border-gray-700-]{+border-edge+} rounded-lg shadow-xl z-50 overflow-hidden">
            <div className="p-2 border-b [-border-gray-700">-]{+border-edge">+}
              <input value={search} onChange={e => setSearch(e.target.value)} placeholder="Search locations..."
                className="w-full h-8 px-2 [-bg-gray-900-]{+bg-bg+} border [-border-gray-600-]{+border-edge-strong+} rounded text-sm [-text-gray-50-]{+text-primary+} focus:outline-none" autoFocus />
            </div>
            <div className="max-h-64 overflow-y-auto">
              <button onClick={() => { setLocation(null); setOpen(false); setSearch('') }}
                className={`w-full text-left px-3 py-2 text-sm [-hover:bg-gray-700-]{+hover:bg-raised+} ${!locationId ? [-'text-emerald-400-]{+'text-accent+} font-medium' : [-'text-gray-300'}`}>-]{+'text-secondary'}`}>+}
                All Locations
              </button>
              {filtered.map(l => (
                <button key={l.id} onClick={() => { setLocation(l.id, l.name); setOpen(false); setSearch('') }}
                  className={`w-full text-left px-3 py-2 text-sm [-hover:bg-gray-700-]{+hover:bg-raised+} ${locationId === l.id ? [-'text-emerald-400-]{+'text-accent+} font-medium' : [-'text-gray-300'}`}>-]{+'text-secondary'}`}>+}
                  {l.name}
                  <span className="text-xs [-text-gray-500-]{+text-muted+} ml-1">— {l.city}</span>
                </button>
              ))}
            </div>
          </div>
        </>
      )}
    </div>
  )
}
